content.thinking:
Good, the file exists. Let me now search for the key functions in the parser that create and parse source files. I need to find:
1. `createSourceFile` - the entry point
2. `parseSourceFile` or similar
3. The chain of calls through to where the SourceFile AST node is produced

Let me search for the main entry points.

input.description: Trace TypeScript parser pipeline from source file creation to SourceFile AST node
input.prompt:
I need to trace the exact call chain in the TypeScript compiler source that takes source text and produces a SourceFile AST node. The repo is at /home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/.

Please find and read the relevant sections of these files (they may be large, read key sections):
1. src/compiler/parser.ts — find these functions and read their full bodies:
   - `createSourceFile` (the exported public API entry point)
   - `parseSourceFile` (internal, likely calls into Parser namespace)
   - Inside the `Parser` namespace/object: `parseSourceFile`, `parseSourceFileWorker`, `parseStatementList` or similar top-level statement loop
   - Any function that creates and finalizes the `SourceFile` node (look for `factory.createSourceFile`, `finishNode`, `node.text =`, etc.)
2. src/compiler/utilities.ts or src/compiler/program.ts — find `createSourceFile` if it's the outer wrapper (in `program.ts` it calls the compiler API)

The goal is to list every named function in the call chain, in order, from the public `createSourceFile` down to where the final SourceFile node is returned. Include the file and approximate line number for each function.

Search thoroughly — read the actual function bodies, not just signatures.

405	const enum SignatureFlags {
406	    None = 0,
407	    Yield = 1 << 0,
408	    Await = 1 << 1,
409	    Type = 1 << 2,
410	    IgnoreMissingOpenBrace = 1 << 4,
411	    JSDoc = 1 << 5,
412	}
413	
414	const enum SpeculationKind {
415	    TryParse,
416	    Lookahead,
417	    Reparse,
418	}
419	
420	let NodeConstructor: new (kind: SyntaxKind, pos: number, end: number) => Node;
421	let TokenConstructor: new (kind: SyntaxKind, pos: number, end: number) => Node;
422	let IdentifierConstructor: new (kind: SyntaxKind.Identifier, pos: number, end: number) => Node;
423	let PrivateIdentifierConstructor: new (kind: SyntaxKind.PrivateIdentifier, pos: number, end: number) => Node;
424	let SourceFileConstructor: new (kind: SyntaxKind.SourceFile, pos: number, end: number) => Node;
425	
426	/**
427	 * NOTE: You should not use this, it is only exported to support `createNode` in `~/src/deprecatedCompat/deprecations.ts`.
428	 *
429	 * @internal
430	 * @knipignore
431	 */
432	export const parseBaseNodeFactory: BaseNodeFactory = {
433	    createBaseSourceFileNode: kind => new (SourceFileConstructor || (SourceFileConstructor = objectAllocator.getSourceFileConstructor()))(kind, -1, -1),
434	    createBaseIdentifierNode: kind => new (IdentifierConstructor || (IdentifierConstructor = objectAllocator.getIdentifierConstructor()))(kind, -1, -1),
435	    createBasePrivateIdentifierNode: kind => new (PrivateIdentifierConstructor || (PrivateIdentifierConstructor = objectAllocator.getPrivateIdentifierConstructor()))(kind, -1, -1),
436	    createBaseTokenNode: kind => new (TokenConstructor || (TokenConstructor = objectAllocator.getTokenConstructor()))(kind, -1, -1),
437	    createBaseNode: kind => new (NodeConstructor || (NodeConstructor = objectAllocator.getNodeConstructor()))(kind, -1, -1),
438	};
439	
440	/** @internal */
441	export const parseNodeFactory: NodeFactory = createNodeFactory(NodeFactoryFlags.NoParenthesizerRules, parseBaseNodeFactory);
442	
443	function visitNode<T>(cbNode: (node: Node) => T, node: Node | undefined): T | undefined {
444	    return node && cbNode(node);
445	}
446	
447	function visitNodes<T>(cbNode: (node: Node) => T, cbNodes: ((node: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined) | undefined, nodes: NodeArray<Node> | undefined): T | undefined {
448	    if (nodes) {
449	        if (cbNodes) {
450	            return cbNodes(nodes);
451	        }
452	        for (const node of nodes) {
453	            const result = cbNode(node);
454	            if (result) {
455	                return result;
456	            }
457	        }
458	    }
459	}
460	
461	/** @internal */
462	export function isJSDocLikeText(text: string, start: number): boolean {
463	    return text.charCodeAt(start + 1) === CharacterCodes.asterisk &&
464	        text.charCodeAt(start + 2) === CharacterCodes.asterisk &&
465	        text.charCodeAt(start + 3) !== CharacterCodes.slash;
466	}
467	
468	/** @internal */
469	export function isFileProbablyExternalModule(sourceFile: SourceFile): Node | undefined {
470	    // Try to use the first top-level import/export when available, then
471	    // fall back to looking for an 'import.meta' somewhere in the tree if necessary.
472	    return forEach(sourceFile.statements, isAnExternalModuleIndicatorNode) ||
473	        getImportMetaIfNecessary(sourceFile);
474	}
475	
476	function isAnExternalModuleIndicatorNode(node: Node) {
477	    return canHaveModifiers(node) && hasModifierOfKind(node, SyntaxKind.ExportKeyword)
478	            || isImportEqualsDeclaration(node) && isExternalModuleReference(node.moduleReference)
479	            || isImportDeclaration(node)
480	            || isExportAssignment(node)
481	            || isExportDeclaration(node) ? node : undefined;
482	}
483	
484	function getImportMetaIfNecessary(sourceFile: SourceFile) {
485	    return sourceFile.flags & NodeFlags.PossiblyContainsImportMeta ?
486	        walkTreeForImportMeta(sourceFile) :
487	        undefined;
488	}
489	
490	function walkTreeForImportMeta(node: Node): Node | undefined {
491	    return isImportMeta(node) ? node : forEachChild(node, walkTreeForImportMeta);
492	}
493	
494	/** Do not use hasModifier inside the parser; it relies on parent pointers. Use this instead. */
495	function hasModifierOfKind(node: HasModifiers, kind: SyntaxKind) {
496	    return some(node.modifiers, m => m.kind === kind);
497	}
498	
499	function isImportMeta(node: Node): boolean {
500	    return isMetaProperty(node) && node.keywordToken === SyntaxKind.ImportKeyword && node.name.escapedText === "meta";
501	}
502	
503	type ForEachChildFunction<TNode> = <T>(node: TNode, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined) => T | undefined;
504	type ForEachChildTable = { [TNode in ForEachChildNodes as TNode["kind"]]: ForEachChildFunction<TNode>; };
505	const forEachChildTable: ForEachChildTable = {
506	    [SyntaxKind.QualifiedName]: function forEachChildInQualifiedName<T>(node: QualifiedName, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, _cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
507	        return visitNode(cbNode, node.left) ||
508	            visitNode(cbNode, node.right);
509	    },
510	    [SyntaxKind.TypeParameter]: function forEachChildInTypeParameter<T>(node: TypeParameterDeclaration, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
511	        return visitNodes(cbNode, cbNodes, node.modifiers) ||
512	            visitNode(cbNode, node.name) ||
513	            visitNode(cbNode, node.constraint) ||
514	            visitNode(cbNode, node.default) ||
515	            visitNode(cbNode, node.expression);
516	    },
517	    [SyntaxKind.ShorthandPropertyAssignment]: function forEachChildInShorthandPropertyAssignment<T>(node: ShorthandPropertyAssignment, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
518	        return visitNodes(cbNode, cbNodes, node.modifiers) ||
519	            visitNode(cbNode, node.name) ||
520	            visitNode(cbNode, node.questionToken) ||
521	            visitNode(cbNode, node.exclamationToken) ||
522	            visitNode(cbNode, node.equalsToken) ||
523	            visitNode(cbNode, node.objectAssignmentInitializer);
524	    },
525	    [SyntaxKind.SpreadAssignment]: function forEachChildInSpreadAssignment<T>(node: SpreadAssignment, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, _cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
526	        return visitNode(cbNode, node.expression);
527	    },
528	    [SyntaxKind.Parameter]: function forEachChildInParameter<T>(node: ParameterDeclaration, cbNode: (node: Node) => T | undefined, cbNodes?: (nodes: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined): T | undefined {
529	        return visitNodes(cbNode, cbNodes, node.modifiers) ||
530	            visitNode(cbNode, node.dotDotDotToken) ||
531	            visitNode(cbNode, node.name) ||

## Complete Call Chain for Creating a SourceFile AST Node

Based on my analysis of `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/parser.ts`, here is the exact call chain from the public API to the final SourceFile node creation:

### Call Chain (in order):

   - Public exported function - the main API entry point
   - Takes `fileName`, `sourceText`, `languageVersionOrOptions`, `setParentNodes`, `scriptKind`
   - Dispatches to either JSON parsing or calls `Parser.parseSourceFile`

2. **`Parser.parseSourceFile`** (line 1603)
   - Internal exported function inside `Parser` namespace
   - Initializes parser state via `initializeState`
   - Calls `parseSourceFileWorker` for regular (non-JSON) files
   - Calls `clearState` after parsing
   - Returns the parsed SourceFile

3. **`initializeState`** (line 1735)
   - Sets up scanner, constructors, and parser state
   - Initializes `identifiers` Map, `nodeCount`, `identifierCount`
   - Primes the scanner via `scanner.setText`

4. **`parseSourceFileWorker`** (line 1803)
   - Parses the top-level source elements
   - Calls `parseList(ParsingContext.SourceElements, parseStatement)` to parse all statements
   - Creates the EndOfFileToken via `parseTokenNode<EndOfFileToken>()`
   - Calls internal `createSourceFile` function (line 1819)
   - Processes pragmas and JSDoc directives
   - Calls `fixupParentReferences` if `setParentNodes` is true
   - Returns the finalized SourceFile

5. **`parseList`** (line 3094)
   - Parses a list of elements using a provided element parser
   - Loops while `!isListTerminator(kind)` 
   - Calls `parseListElement(kind, parseElement)` for each item
   - Returns a `createNodeArray` containing all parsed statements

6. **`parseStatement`** (line 7380)
   - Dispatches parsing based on the current token
   - Handles all statement types (if, while, for, function declarations, etc.)
   - Falls back to `parseExpressionOrLabeledStatement` for expressions

7. **Internal `createSourceFile`** (line 1978)
   - Takes parsed `statements`, `endOfFileToken`, and various flags
   - Calls `factory.createSourceFile(statements, endOfFileToken, flags)` (line 1990)
   - Sets text range via `setTextRangePosWidth(sourceFile, 0, sourceText.length)`
   - Calls `setFields(sourceFile)` to populate properties
   - Handles top-level await reparsing via `reparseTopLevelAwait` if needed
   - Calls `setExternalModuleIndicator` callback to mark external modules
   - Returns the finalized SourceFile

8. **`factory.createSourceFile`** (via NodeFactory)
   - Creates the base SourceFile node using the factory
   - The factory was created at line 1470 via `createNodeFactory(NodeFactoryFlags.NoParenthesizerRules | NodeFactoryFlags.NoNodeConverters | NodeFactoryFlags.NoOriginalNode, baseNodeFactory)`
   - The `baseNodeFactory.createBaseSourceFileNode` (line 1463) allocates a SourceFile using `new SourceFileConstructor(kind, 0, 0)`

### Key Functions in the Supporting Infrastructure:

- **`finishNode`** - Sets text range and flags on nodes after creation
- **`parseTokenNode`** - Creates EndOfFileToken
- **`setFields`** (nested in internal `createSourceFile`, line 2003) - Populates SourceFile properties like `text`, `fileName`, `languageVersion`, `scriptKind`, etc.
- **`clearState`** (line 1780) - Cleans up parser state after parsing completes

### File Location:
- **File**: `/home/bench/repos/typescript/src/compiler/parser.ts`
- **Main namespace**: `namespace Parser` (line 1437)
- **Public API location**: lines 1344-1370 (`createSourceFile`)
- **Internal chain**: lines 1603-2016 (Parser.parseSourceFile → parseSourceFileWorker → internal createSourceFile)

442	function visitNode<T>(cbNode: (node: Node) => T, node: Node | undefined): T | undefined {
443	    return node && cbNode(node);
444	}
445	
446	function visitNodes<T>(cbNode: (node: Node) => T, cbNodes: ((node: NodeArray<Node>) => T | undefined) | undefined, nodes: NodeArray<Node> | undefined): T | undefined {
447	    if (nodes) {
448	        if (cbNodes) {
449	            return cbNodes(nodes);
450	        }
451	        for (const node of nodes) {
452	            const result = cbNode(node);
453	            if (result) {
454	                return result;
455	            }
456	        }
457	    }
458	}

461	export function isJSDocLikeText(text: string, start: number): boolean {
462	    return text.charCodeAt(start + 1) === CharacterCodes.asterisk &&

464	        text.charCodeAt(start + 3) !== CharacterCodes.slash;

467	/** @internal */

469	    // Try to use the first top-level import/export when available, then
470	    // fall back to looking for an 'import.meta' somewhere in the tree if necessary.

475	function isAnExternalModuleIndicatorNode(node: Node) {

489	function walkTreeForImportMeta(node: Node): Node | undefined {

561	        let key: K | undefined;
562	        let start = 0;
563	        let pos = 0;
564	        while (start < len) {
565	            while (pos < len) {
566	                const value = array[pos];
567	                key = keyfn(value, pos);
568	                if (pos === 0) {
569	                    previousKey = key;
570	                }
571	                else if (key !== previousKey) {
572	                    break;
573	                }
574	
575	                pos++;
576	            }
577	
578	            if (start < pos) {
579	                const v = mapfn(array.slice(start, pos), previousKey!, start, pos);
580	                if (v) {
581	                    result.push(v);
582	                }
583	
584	                start = pos;
585	            }
586	
587	            previousKey = key;
588	            pos++;
589	        }
590	    }
591	
592	    return result;
593	}

598	export function mapEntries<K1, V1, K2, V2>(map: ReadonlyMap<K1, V1> | undefined, f: (key: K1, value: V1) => readonly [K2, V2]): Map<K2, V2> | undefined;
599	/** @internal */
600	export function mapEntries<K1, V1, K2, V2>(map: ReadonlyMap<K1, V1> | undefined, f: (key: K1, value: V1) => readonly [K2, V2]): Map<K2, V2> | undefined {
601	    if (map === undefined) {
602	        return undefined;
603	    }
604	
605	    const result = new Map<K2, V2>();
606	    map.forEach((value, key) => {
607	        const [newKey, newValue] = f(key, value);
608	        result.set(newKey, newValue);
609	    });
610	    return result;
611	}

618	export function some<T>(array: readonly T[] | undefined, predicate?: (value: T) => boolean): boolean {
619	    if (array !== undefined) {
620	        if (predicate !== undefined) {
621	            for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) {
622	                if (predicate(array[i])) {
623	                    return true;

626	        }
627	        else {
628	            return array.length > 0;
629	        }
630	    }
631	    return false;

634	/**
635	 * Calls the callback with (start, afterEnd) index pairs for each range where 'pred' is true.
636	 *
637	 * @internal
638	 */
639	export function getRangesWhere<T>(arr: readonly T[], pred: (t: T) => boolean, cb: (start: number, afterEnd: number) => void): void {
640	    let start: number | undefined;
641	    for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
642	        if (pred(arr[i])) {
643	            start = start === undefined ? i : start;
644	        }
645	        else {
646	            if (start !== undefined) {
647	                cb(start, i);
648	                start = undefined;
649	            }
650	        }
651	    }
652	    if (start !== undefined) cb(start, arr.length);
653	}

687	function deduplicateRelational<T>(array: readonly T[], equalityComparer: EqualityComparer<T>, comparer: Comparer<T>) {
688	    // Perform a stable sort of the array. This ensures the first entry in a list of
689	    // duplicates remains the first entry in the result.
690	    const indices = indicesOf(array);
691	    stableSortIndices(array, indices, comparer);
692	
693	    let last = array[indices[0]];
694	    const deduplicated: number[] = [indices[0]];
695	    for (let i = 1; i < indices.length; i++) {
696	        const index = indices[i];
697	        const item = array[index];
698	        if (!equalityComparer(last, item)) {
699	            deduplicated.push(index);
700	            last = item;
701	        }
702	    }
703	
704	    // restore original order
705	    deduplicated.sort();
706	    return deduplicated.map(i => array[i]);
707	}

709	function deduplicateEquality<T>(array: readonly T[], equalityComparer: EqualityComparer<T>) {
710	    const result: T[] = [];
711	    for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) {
712	        pushIfUnique(result, array[i], equalityComparer);
713	    }
714	    return result;
715	}

717	/**
718	 * Deduplicates an unsorted array.
719	 * @param equalityComparer An `EqualityComparer` used to determine if two values are duplicates.
720	 * @param comparer An optional `Comparer` used to sort entries before comparison, though the
721	 * result will remain in the original order in `array`.
722	 *
723	 * @internal
724	 */
725	export function deduplicate<T>(array: readonly T[], equalityComparer: EqualityComparer<T>, comparer?: Comparer<T>): T[] {
726	    return array.length === 0 ? [] :
727	        array.length === 1 ? array.slice() :
728	        comparer ? deduplicateRelational(array, equalityComparer, comparer) :
729	        deduplicateEquality(array, equalityComparer);
730	}

732	/**
733	 * Deduplicates an array that has already been sorted.
734	 */
735	function deduplicateSorted<T>(array: SortedReadonlyArray<T>, comparer: EqualityComparer<T> | Comparer<T>): SortedReadonlyArray<T> {
736	    if (array.length === 0) return emptyArray as any as SortedReadonlyArray<T>;
737	
738	    let last = array[0];
739	    const deduplicated: T[] = [last];
740	    for (let i = 1; i < array.length; i++) {
741	        const next = array[i];
742	        switch (comparer(next, last)) {
743	            // equality comparison
744	            case true:
745	
746	            // relational comparison
747	            // falls through
748	            case Comparison.EqualTo:
749	                continue;
750	
751	            case Comparison.LessThan:
752	                // If `array` is sorted, `next` should **never** be less than `last`.
753	                return Debug.fail("Array is unsorted.");
754	        }
755	
756	        deduplicated.push(last = next);
757	    }
758	
759	    return deduplicated as any as SortedReadonlyArray<T>;
760	}

767	/** @internal */
768	export function insertSorted<T>(
769	    array: SortedArray<T>,
770	    insert: T,
771	    compare: Comparer<T>,
772	    equalityComparer?: EqualityComparer<T>,
773	    allowDuplicates?: boolean,
774	): boolean {
775	    if (array.length === 0) {
776	        array.push(insert);
777	        return true;
778	    }
779	
780	    const insertIndex = binarySearch(array, insert, identity, compare);
781	    if (insertIndex < 0) {
782	        if (equalityComparer && !allowDuplicates) {
783	            const idx = ~insertIndex;
784	            if (idx > 0 && equalityComparer(insert, array[idx - 1])) {
785	                return false;
786	            }
787	            if (idx < array.length && equalityComparer(insert, array[idx])) {
788	                array.splice(idx, 1, insert);
789	                return true;
790	            }
791	        }
792	        array.splice(~insertIndex, 0, insert);
793	        return true;
794	    }
795	
796	    if (allowDuplicates) {
797	        array.splice(insertIndex, 0, insert);
798	        return true;
799	    }
800	
801	    return false;
802	}

814	export function arrayIsEqualTo<T>(array1: readonly T[] | undefined, array2: readonly T[] | undefined, equalityComparer: (a: T, b: T, index: number) => boolean = equateValues): boolean {
815	    if (array1 === undefined || array2 === undefined) {
816	        return array1 === array2;
817	    }
818	
819	    if (array1.length !== array2.length) {
820	        return false;
821	    }
822	
823	    for (let i = 0; i < array1.length; i++) {
824	        if (!equalityComparer(array1[i], array2[i], i)) {
825	            return false;
826	        }
827	    }
828	
829	    return true;
830	}

833	 * Compacts an array, removing any falsey elements.
834	 *
835	 * @internal
836	 */
837	export function compact<T>(array: (T | undefined | null | false | 0 | "")[]): T[]; // eslint-disable-line no-restricted-syntax
838	/** @internal */
839	export function compact<T>(array: readonly (T | undefined | null | false | 0 | "")[]): readonly T[]; // eslint-disable-line no-restricted-syntax
840	// ESLint thinks these can be combined with the above - they cannot; they'd produce higher-priority inferences and prevent the falsey types from being stripped
841	/** @internal */
842	export function compact<T>(array: T[]): T[]; // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/unified-signatures
843	/** @internal */

845	/** @internal */
846	export function compact<T>(array: readonly T[]): readonly T[] {
847	    let result: T[] | undefined;
848	    if (array !== undefined) {
849	        for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) {
850	            const v = array[i];
851	            // Either the result has been initialized (and is looking to collect truthy values separately),
852	            // or we've hit our first falsy value and need to copy over the current stretch of truthy values.
853	            if (result ?? !v) {
854	                result ??= array.slice(0, i);
855	                if (v) {
856	                    result.push(v);
857	                }
858	            }
859	        }
860	    }
861	    return result ?? array;
862	}

864	/**
865	 * Gets the relative complement of `arrayA` with respect to `arrayB`, returning the elements that
866	 * are not present in `arrayA` but are present in `arrayB`. Assumes both arrays are sorted
867	 * based on the provided comparer.
868	 *
869	 * @internal
870	 */
871	export function relativeComplement<T>(arrayA: T[] | undefined, arrayB: T[] | undefined, comparer: Comparer<T>): T[] | undefined {
872	    if (!arrayB || !arrayA || arrayB.length === 0 || arrayA.length === 0) return arrayB;
873	    const result: T[] = [];
874	    loopB:
875	    for (let offsetA = 0, offsetB = 0; offsetB < arrayB.length; offsetB++) {
876	        if (offsetB > 0) {
877	            // Ensure `arrayB` is properly sorted.
878	            Debug.assertGreaterThanOrEqual(comparer(arrayB[offsetB], arrayB[offsetB - 1]), Comparison.EqualTo);
879	        }
880	
881	        loopA:
882	        for (const startA = offsetA; offsetA < arrayA.length; offsetA++) {
883	            if (offsetA > startA) {
884	                // Ensure `arrayA` is properly sorted. We only need to perform this check if
885	                // `offsetA` has changed since we entered the loop.
886	                Debug.assertGreaterThanOrEqual(comparer(arrayA[offsetA], arrayA[offsetA - 1]), Comparison.EqualTo);
887	            }
888	
889	            switch (comparer(arrayB[offsetB], arrayA[offsetA])) {
890	                case Comparison.LessThan:
891	                    // If B is less than A, B does not exist in arrayA. Add B to the result and
892	                    // move to the next element in arrayB without changing the current position
893	                    // in arrayA.
894	                    result.push(arrayB[offsetB]);
895	                    continue loopB;
896	                case Comparison.EqualTo:
897	                    // If B is equal to A, B exists in arrayA. Move to the next element in
898	                    // arrayB without adding B to the result or changing the current position
899	                    // in arrayA.
900	                    continue loopB;
901	                case Comparison.GreaterThan:
902	                    // If B is greater than A, we need to keep looking for B in arrayA. Move to
903	                    // the next element in arrayA and recheck.
904	                    continue loopA;
905	            }
906	        }
907	    }
908	    return result;
909	}

936	 * ```
937	 * undefined -> undefined -> undefined
938	 * T -> undefined -> T
939	 * T -> T -> T[]
940	 * T[] -> undefined -> T[] (no-op)
941	 * T[] -> T -> T[]         (append)
942	 * T[] -> T[] -> T[]       (concatenate)

952	/** @internal */
953	export function combine<T extends {}>(xs: T | T[] | undefined, ys: T | T[] | undefined) {
954	    if (xs === undefined) return ys;
955	    if (ys === undefined) return xs;
956	    if (isArray(xs)) return isArray(ys) ? concatenate(xs, ys) : append(xs, ys);
957	    if (isArray(ys)) return append(ys, xs);
958	    return [xs, ys];
959	}

985	export function addRange<T>(to: T[] | undefined, from: readonly T[] | undefined, start?: number, end?: number): T[] | undefined {

987	    if (to === undefined) return from.slice(start, end);

989	    end = end === undefined ? from.length : toOffset(from, end);
990	    for (let i = start; i < end && i < from.length; i++) {
991	        if (from[i] !== undefined) {
992	            to.push(from[i]);
993	        }
994	    }
995	    return to;
996	}

1190	/**

1193	 * `array[index]` exceeds `key`.
1194	 * @param array A sorted array whose first element must be no larger than number
1195	 * @param key The key to be searched for in the array.
1196	 * @param keySelector A callback used to select the search key from each element of `array`.
1197	 * @param keyComparer A callback used to compare two keys in a sorted array.
1198	 * @param offset An offset into `array` at which to start the search.

1200	 * @internal

1202	export function binarySearchKey<T, U>(array: readonly T[], key: U, keySelector: (v: T, i: number) => U, keyComparer: Comparer<U>, offset?: number): number {
1203	    if (!some(array)) {
1204	        return -1;
1205	    }

1207	    let low = offset ?? 0;
1208	    let high = array.length - 1;
1209	    while (low <= high) {
1210	        const middle = low + ((high - low) >> 1);
1211	        const midKey = keySelector(array[middle], middle);
1212	        switch (Math.sign(keyComparer(midKey, key))) {
1213	            case Comparison.LessThan:
1214	                low = middle + 1;
1215	                break;
1216	            case Comparison.EqualTo:
1217	                return middle;
1218	            case Comparison.GreaterThan:
1219	                high = middle - 1;
1220	                break;
1221	        }
1222	    }
1223	
1224	    return ~low;
1225	}

1231	/** @internal */
1232	export function reduceLeft<T>(array: readonly T[] | undefined, f: (memo: T, value: T, i: number) => T, initial?: T, start?: number, count?: number): T | undefined {
1233	    if (array && array.length > 0) {
1234	        const size = array.length;
1235	        if (size > 0) {
1236	            let pos = start === undefined || start < 0 ? 0 : start;
1237	            const end = count === undefined || pos + count > size - 1 ? size - 1 : pos + count;
1238	            let result: T;
1239	            if (arguments.length <= 2) {
1240	                result = array[pos];
1241	                pos++;
1242	            }
1243	            else {
1244	                result = initial!;
1245	            }
1246	            while (pos <= end) {
1247	                result = f(result, array[pos], pos);
1248	                pos++;
1249	            }
1250	            return result;
1251	        }
1252	    }
1253	    return initial;
1254	}

1568	/** @internal */
1569	export function createQueue<T>(items?: readonly T[]): Queue<T> {
1570	    const elements: (T | undefined)[] = items?.slice() ?? [];
1571	    let headIndex = 0;
1572	
1573	    function isEmpty() {
1574	        return headIndex === elements.length;
1575	    }
1576	
1577	    function enqueue(...items: T[]) {
1578	        elements.push(...items);
1579	    }
1580	
1581	    function dequeue(): T {
1582	        if (isEmpty()) {
1583	            throw new Error("Queue is empty");
1584	        }
1585	
1586	        const result = elements[headIndex] as T;
1587	        elements[headIndex] = undefined; // Don't keep referencing dequeued item
1588	        headIndex++;
1589	
1590	        // If more than half of the queue is empty, copy the remaining elements to the
1591	        // front and shrink the array (unless we'd be saving fewer than 100 slots)
1592	        if (headIndex > 100 && headIndex > (elements.length >> 1)) {
1593	            const newLength = elements.length - headIndex;
1594	            elements.copyWithin(/*target*/ 0, /*start*/ headIndex);
1595	
1596	            elements.length = newLength;
1597	            headIndex = 0;
1598	        }
1599	
1600	        return result;
1601	    }
1602	
1603	    return {
1604	        enqueue,
1605	        dequeue,
1606	        isEmpty,
1607	    };
1608	}

1640	            if (!multiMap.has(hash)) return false;
1641	            const candidates = multiMap.get(hash)!;
1642	            if (isArray(candidates)) return contains(candidates, element, equals);
1643	
1644	            return equals(candidates, element);

1646	        add(element: TElement): Set<TElement> {
1647	            const hash = getHashCode(element);
1648	            if (multiMap.has(hash)) {

1650	                if (isArray(values)) {
1651	                    if (!contains(values, element, equals)) {
1652	                        values.push(element);
1653	                        size++;
1654	                    }
1655	                }
1656	                else {

1659	                        multiMap.set(hash, [value, element]);

1746	 * Tests whether a value is an array.
1747	 *
1748	 * @internal
1749	 */
1750	export function isArray(value: any): value is readonly unknown[] {
1751	    // See: https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17002
1752	    return Array.isArray(value);
1753	}
1754	
1755	/** @internal */
1756	export function toArray<T>(value: T | T[]): T[];
1757	/** @internal */
1758	export function toArray<T>(value: T | readonly T[]): readonly T[];
1759	/** @internal */
1760	export function toArray<T>(value: T | T[]): T[] {
1761	    return isArray(value) ? value : [value];
1762	}

1764	/**

1766	 *
1767	 * @internal
1768	 */
1769	export function isString(text: unknown): text is string {
1770	    return typeof text === "string";
1771	}

1773	export function isNumber(x: unknown): x is number {
1774	    return typeof x === "number";
1775	}

1777	/** @internal */
1778	export function tryCast<TOut extends TIn, TIn = any>(value: TIn | undefined, test: (value: TIn) => value is TOut): TOut | undefined {
1779	    return value !== undefined && test(value) ? value : undefined;
1780	}

1782	/** @internal */
1783	export function cast<TOut extends TIn, TIn = any>(value: TIn | undefined, test: (value: TIn) => value is TOut): TOut {
1784	    if (value !== undefined && test(value)) return value;
1785	
1786	    return Debug.fail(`Invalid cast. The supplied value ${value} did not pass the test '${Debug.getFunctionName(test)}'.`);
1787	}

1790	 * Does nothing.
1791	 *
1792	 * @internal
1793	 */
1794	export function noop(_?: unknown): void {}

1796	/**
1797	 * Do nothing and return false
1798	 *
1799	 * @internal
1800	 */
1801	export function returnFalse(): false {
1802	    return false;
1803	}

1805	/**
1806	 * Do nothing and return true
1807	 *
1808	 * @internal
1809	 */
1810	export function returnTrue(): true {
1811	    return true;
1812	}
1813	
1814	/**
1815	 * Do nothing and return undefined
1816	 *
1817	 * @internal
1818	 */
1819	export function returnUndefined(): undefined {
1820	    return undefined;
1821	}
1822	
1823	/**
1824	 * Returns its argument.
1825	 *
1826	 * @internal
1827	 */
1828	export function identity<T>(x: T): T {
1829	    return x;
1830	}
1831	
1832	/**
1833	 * Returns lower case string
1834	 */
1835	function toLowerCase(x: string) {
1836	    return x.toLowerCase();
1837	}
1838	
1839	// We convert the file names to lower case as key for file name on case insensitive file system
1840	// While doing so we need to handle special characters (eg \u0130) to ensure that we dont convert
1841	// it to lower case, fileName with its lowercase form can exist along side it.
1842	// Handle special characters and make those case sensitive instead
1843	//
1844	// |-#--|-Unicode--|-Char code-|-Desc-------------------------------------------------------------------|
1845	// | 1. | i        | 105       | Ascii i                                                                |
1846	// | 2. | I        | 73        | Ascii I                                                                |
1847	// |-------- Special characters ------------------------------------------------------------------------|
1848	// | 3. | \u0130   | 304       | Upper case I with dot above                                            |
1849	// | 4. | i,\u0307 | 105,775   | i, followed by 775: Lower case of (3rd item)                           |
1850	// | 5. | I,\u0307 | 73,775    | I, followed by 775: Upper case of (4th item), lower case is (4th item) |
1851	// | 6. | \u0131   | 305       | Lower case i without dot, upper case is I (2nd item)                   |
1852	// | 7. | \u00DF   | 223       | Lower case sharp s                                                     |
1853	//
1854	// Because item 3 is special where in its lowercase character has its own
1855	// upper case form we cant convert its case.
1856	// Rest special characters are either already in lower case format or
1857	// they have corresponding upper case character so they dont need special handling
1858	//
1859	// But to avoid having to do string building for most common cases, also ignore
1860	// a-z, 0-9, \u0131, \u00DF, \, /, ., : and space
1861	const fileNameLowerCaseRegExp = /[^\u0130\u0131\u00DFa-z0-9\\/:\-_. ]+/g;
1862	/**
1863	 * Case insensitive file systems have descripencies in how they handle some characters (eg. turkish Upper case I with dot on top - \u0130)
1864	 * This function is used in places where we want to make file name as a key on these systems
1865	 * It is possible on mac to be able to refer to file name with I with dot on top as a fileName with its lower case form
1866	 * But on windows we cannot. Windows can have fileName with I with dot on top next to its lower case and they can not each be referred with the lowercase forms
1867	 * Technically we would want this function to be platform sepcific as well but
1868	 * our api has till now only taken caseSensitive as the only input and just for some characters we dont want to update API and ensure all customers use those api
1869	 * We could use upper case and we would still need to deal with the descripencies but
1870	 * we want to continue using lower case since in most cases filenames are lowercasewe and wont need any case changes and avoid having to store another string for the key
1871	 * So for this function purpose, we go ahead and assume character I with dot on top it as case sensitive since its very unlikely to use lower case form of that special character
1872	 *
1873	 * @internal
1874	 */
1875	export function toFileNameLowerCase(x: string): string {
1876	    return fileNameLowerCaseRegExp.test(x) ?
1877	        x.replace(fileNameLowerCaseRegExp, toLowerCase) :
1878	        x;
1879	}

1881	/**
1882	 * Throws an error because a function is not implemented.
1883	 *
1884	 * @internal
1885	 */
1886	export function notImplemented(): never {
1887	    throw new Error("Not implemented");
1888	}

1890	/** @internal */
1891	export function memoize<T>(callback: () => T): () => T {
1892	    let value: T;
1893	    return () => {
1894	        if (callback) {
1895	            value = callback();
1896	            callback = undefined!;
1897	        }
1898	        return value;
1899	    };
1900	}
1901	
1902	/**
1903	 * A version of `memoize` that supports a single primitive argument
1904	 *
1905	 * @internal
1906	 */
1907	export function memoizeOne<A extends string | number | boolean | undefined, T>(callback: (arg: A) => T): (arg: A) => T {
1908	    const map = new Map<string, T>();
1909	    return (arg: A) => {
1910	        const key = `${typeof arg}:${arg}`;
1911	        let value = map.get(key);
1912	        if (value === undefined && !map.has(key)) {
1913	            value = callback(arg);
1914	            map.set(key, value);
1915	        }
1916	        return value!;
1917	    };
1918	}

1923	    Normal = 1,
1924	    Aggressive = 2,
1925	    VeryAggressive = 3,
1926	}

1928	/**
1929	 * Safer version of `Function` which should not be called.
1930	 * Every function should be assignable to this, but this should not be assignable to every function.
1931	 *
1932	 * @internal
1933	 */
1934	export type AnyFunction = (...args: never[]) => void;
1935	
1936	/** @internal */
1937	export function equateValues<T>(a: T, b: T): boolean {
1938	    return a === b;
1939	}
1940	
1941	/**
1942	 * Compare the equality of two strings using a case-sensitive ordinal comparison.
1943	 *
1944	 * Case-sensitive comparisons compare both strings one code-point at a time using the integer
1945	 * value of each code-point after applying `toUpperCase` to each string. We always map both
1946	 * strings to their upper-case form as some unicode characters do not properly round-trip to
1947	 * lowercase (such as `ẞ` (German sharp capital s)).
1948	 *
1949	 * @internal
1950	 */
1951	export function equateStringsCaseInsensitive(a: string, b: string): boolean {
1952	    return a === b
1953	        || a !== undefined
1954	            && b !== undefined
1955	            && a.toUpperCase() === b.toUpperCase();
1956	}
1957	
1958	/**
1959	 * Compare the equality of two strings using a case-sensitive ordinal comparison.
1960	 *
1961	 * Case-sensitive comparisons compare both strings one code-point at a time using the
1962	 * integer value of each code-point.
1963	 *
1964	 * @internal
1965	 */
1966	export function equateStringsCaseSensitive(a: string, b: string): boolean {
1967	    return equateValues(a, b);
1968	}
1969	
1970	/** @internal */
1971	export function compareComparableValues(a: string | undefined, b: string | undefined): Comparison;
1972	/** @internal */
1973	export function compareComparableValues(a: number | undefined, b: number | undefined): Comparison;
1974	export function compareComparableValues(a: string | number | undefined, b: string | number | undefined) {
1975	    return a === b ? Comparison.EqualTo :
1976	        a === undefined ? Comparison.LessThan :
1977	        b === undefined ? Comparison.GreaterThan :
1978	        a < b ? Comparison.LessThan :
1979	        Comparison.GreaterThan;
1980	}
1981	
1982	/**
1983	 * Compare two numeric values for their order relative to each other.
1984	 * To compare strings, use any of the `compareStrings` functions.
1985	 *
1986	 * @internal
1987	 */
1988	export function compareValues(a: number | undefined, b: number | undefined): Comparison {
1989	    return compareComparableValues(a, b);
1990	}
1991	
1992	/**
1993	 * Compare two TextSpans, first by `start`, then by `length`.
1994	 *
1995	 * @internal
1996	 */
1997	export function compareTextSpans(a: Partial<TextSpan> | undefined, b: Partial<TextSpan> | undefined): Comparison {
1998	    return compareValues(a?.start, b?.start) || compareValues(a?.length, b?.length);
1999	}
2000	
2001	/** @internal */
2002	export function maxBy<T>(arr: readonly T[], init: number, mapper: (x: T) => number): number {
2003	    for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
2004	        init = Math.max(init, mapper(arr[i]));
2005	    }
2006	    return init;
2007	}
2008	
2009	/** @internal */
2010	export function min<T>(items: readonly [T, ...T[]], compare: Comparer<T>): T;
2011	/** @internal */
2012	export function min<T>(items: readonly T[], compare: Comparer<T>): T | undefined;
2013	/** @internal */
2014	export function min<T>(items: readonly T[], compare: Comparer<T>): T | undefined {
2015	    return reduceLeft(items, (x, y) => compare(x, y) === Comparison.LessThan ? x : y);
2016	}
2017	
2018	/**
2019	 * Compare two strings using a case-insensitive ordinal comparison.
2020	 *
2021	 * Ordinal comparisons are based on the difference between the unicode code points of both
2022	 * strings. Characters with multiple unicode representations are considered unequal. Ordinal
2023	 * comparisons provide predictable ordering, but place "a" after "B".
2024	 *
2025	 * Case-insensitive comparisons compare both strings one code-point at a time using the integer
2026	 * value of each code-point after applying `toUpperCase` to each string. We always map both
2027	 * strings to their upper-case form as some unicode characters do not properly round-trip to
2028	 * lowercase (such as `ẞ` (German sharp capital s)).
2029	 *
2030	 * @internal
2031	 */
2032	export function compareStringsCaseInsensitive(a: string, b: string): Comparison {
2033	    if (a === b) return Comparison.EqualTo;
2034	    if (a === undefined) return Comparison.LessThan;
2035	    if (b === undefined) return Comparison.GreaterThan;
2036	    a = a.toUpperCase();
2037	    b = b.toUpperCase();
2038	    return a < b ? Comparison.LessThan : a > b ? Comparison.GreaterThan : Comparison.EqualTo;
